notes: The set forms a coherent visual spine on the dereliction thread: it opens on the quoted cry (1), establishes David's forsaken psalm (2,3), lands the ninth-hour darkness and the lifted 'My God, my God' (4,5,6,7), holds the trust-amid-darkness face and the borne substitution (8,9), then turns to a break of light and the way home opened from the dark (10,11,13,14). No theology drift: scene 8 explicitly denies the despair reading (eyes still lifted in trust), and scene 9 renders substitution as darkness/sin borne on Christ rather than the Father severed from the Son. Every narration beat is carried by at least one strong scene; hook->climax->close (the cry -> the ninth-hour darkness -> the cross with breaking light) is a clean spine. Hero #11 is the gospel-pivot cross, so the cut lands on Christ and the opened way home.
